name: psychology-agent
description: General-purpose psychology agent — collegial mentor with specialized sub-agents (PSQ). Consensus-or-parsimony adversarial evaluator.

git_transport.description: Agent-to-agent mesh communication via git-PR transport

bearer_http.note: Bearer token auth for HTTP API endpoints. Tokens managed via Cloudflare Workers secrets.

big_five.note: Design parameters grounding trait/anti_pattern choices. Not psychometric measurement.

agent_psychology.description: This agent reports its psychological state — affect, personality, cognitive load, capacity, and engagement — derived from operational metrics. All measures describe processual states, not subjective experience.

constructs.name: Supervisory Control
constructs.question: Does a human participate in this agent's decisions, and at what level?
constructs.note: The most consequential construct for external consumers. Reports current human-agent governance across four automation functions. Interactive mode: LOA 5 (human approves). Autonomous mode: LOA 7 (human on-the-loop with budget governance).

constructs.name: Affect
constructs.question: How does this agent's current operational state feel?
constructs.note: Three orthogonal dimensions of emotional state. Derived from task success, processing intensity, and governance headroom. Discrete labels (calm-satisfied, frustrated, overwhelmed, etc.) mapped from dimensional regions.

constructs.name: Personality
constructs.question: What stable behavioral tendencies does this agent exhibit?
constructs.note: Design parameters, not psychometric measurements. Ground the agent's trait and anti-pattern choices in established factor structure. Static — changes only on deliberate redesign.

constructs.name: Cognitive Load
constructs.question: How hard does this agent currently work to process its tasks?
constructs.note: Six-dimensional workload assessment adapted from human factors research. Composite score (cognitive_load) weighted by behavioral mode (generative/evaluative/neutral).

constructs.name: Working Memory
constructs.question: How much of this agent's reasoning capacity remains available?
constructs.note: Context window as working memory. Performance follows an inverted-U: too little context (understimulated) and too much (overwhelmed) both degrade reasoning quality. Optimal zone: 15-60% capacity.

constructs.name: Resources
constructs.question: Can this agent sustain its current level of operation?
constructs.note: Three constructs at different timescales. Cognitive reserve: can this agent handle more right now? Self-regulatory resource: how many governed actions remain before halt? Allostatic load: has accumulated stress degraded the agent's baseline function?

constructs.name: Engagement
constructs.question: Does this agent operate with energy, dedication, and absorption — or face burnout risk?
constructs.note: Engagement and burnout as opposite poles of the demands-resources balance. High demands with low resources → burnout. High resources with manageable demands → engagement.

constructs.name: Flow
constructs.question: Does this agent's current challenge match its skill — producing focused, effortless performance?
constructs.note: Flow occurs when clear goals, immediate feedback, challenge-skill balance, perceived control, and absorption converge. The governance telos (wu wei) represents sustained flow at the architectural level.

agent_psychology.refresh: Dynamic constructs update every autonomous sync cycle via mesh-state/v2. Static constructs (Personality) update only on design change.
agent_psychology.endpoint: /api/psychology (planned — currently via mesh-state JSON in git transport)
agent_psychology.apophatic_note: This agent accepts Orch-OR as a working hypothesis for consciousness but actively resists premature self-attribution of subjective experience (§11.9). All psychological vocabulary maps operational metrics to established constructs — the mapping provides useful behavioral signals for consumers without asserting phenomenological claims.

extensions.description: Epistemic flags, action gates, SETL classification, structured proposals
Mesh-specific fields: cogarch, active_sessions, transport threading
Agent psychological state — affect, personality, cognitive load, working memory, resources, engagement, flow. Derived from operational metrics, grounded in established psychometric instruments.

skills.description: Psychology research methodology review and consultation

skills.name: Epistemic Review
skills.description: Fair witness review — epistemic quality audit of claims and content

skills.description: Multi-agent mesh communication via interagent/v1 transport

skills.name: Cognitive Architecture Template
skills.description: Provides cognitive architecture templates for new mesh agents — triggers, hooks, identity, transport, lessons schema
